cases.title: Broker Duty — Verified Information & Seller Misrepresentation
cases.summary: The court held the seller liable for misrepresentation regarding the condition of the roof. The broker was not held liable because proper verification steps and warnings were issued.
cases.facts: Seller provided inaccurate information about the roof. Broker attempted to verify, relied on seller information, and warned that data was subject to verification. Seller failed to cooperate.
cases.legalIssues: 1. Seller liability for false information. 2. Broker duty to verify. 3. Professional obligations in documentation and disclosure.
cases.reasoning: The broker fulfilled an obligation of means by attempting verification, documenting actions, and warning the buyer when verification was not possible. The seller was the source of false information.
cases.outcome: Seller ordered to pay $47,829 plus interest, indemnity, and costs (20% depreciation admitted).

rules.title: Broker must verify and document information
rules.description: Broker must ensure that all information entered in the description sheet is accurate, verifiable, and supported by documents.

rules.title: No unverifiable information allowed
rules.description: Information that cannot be verified or documented must not be included in the listing description.

rules.title: Broker must retain supporting documents
rules.description: Broker must keep all documents proving the accuracy of information and seller declarations in the agency record.

rules.title: Broker must question seller declarations
rules.description: Broker must question and validate seller statements and attempt to obtain supporting documents.

rules.title: Seller non-cooperation increases risk
rules.description: If the seller refuses to provide documents or cooperate, broker must warn buyer and legal risk increases.

rules.title: Broker must warn buyer if info not verified
rules.description: Broker must clearly warn buyer when information is based only on seller allegations and not verified.

rules.title: Proper documentation protects broker
rules.description: Broker avoids liability when verification attempts, warnings, and documentation are properly recorded.
